Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly states PSEP1 is encoded by lncRNA-sORF Pp3c9_sORF1544.

Source_Species.reason: The study organism is moss throughout; specifically PSEP1 was studied in moss.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly studies Physcomitrella patens.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests were performed in moss.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Overexpression and knockout analyses were done in Physcomitrella patens.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify Physcomitrella patens subsp. patens 'Gransden 2004'.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The source organism used in the paper is the same 'Gransden 2004' line.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PSEP1 is described as a peptide encoded by an lncRNA-sORF; 'sORF-encoded peptide; lncRNA-encoded small peptide' is consistent with the paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state that PSEP1 overexpression caused longer caulonema cells and rapid growth with earlier aging/cell death, while knockout showed a small but significant growth difference under some conditions.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper reports MS confirmation of translation, generation of overexpression lines, CRISPR/Cas9 knockout lines, and morphological/growth-rate phenotyping.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point matches Results: 41-aa lncRNA-encoded peptide, overexpression increased caulonema length and growth, knockout gave modest growth defects in some conditions.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results mention BCD medium with 0.5% glucose for PSEP1 phenotypes and note differences on solid media and in liquid culture without glucose.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Pp3c9_sORF1544 is listed among three SEPs common to all three cell types: gametophores, protonemata, and protoplasts.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Same support as applied species: the peptide was detected in all three moss cell types.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state overexpression constructs were made in pPLV27/pPLV-Hpa-4FR, but no promoter is explicitly named; this field correctly says promoter not explicitly stated.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results specifically say rapid growth in PSEP1 overexpressing lines was accompanied by earlier aging and cell death.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly defines PSEP1 as 'Physcomitrella patens sORF encoded peptide 1' and says it is encoded by an lncRNA-sORF.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Methods and Results identify PSEP3 as Pp3c25_sORF1253.

Source_Species.reason: The peptide was studied in moss.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses were conducted in moss.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Manipulations were done in Physcomitrella patens.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The source material in the paper is also 'Gransden 2004'.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper describes Pp3c25_sORF1253 as a conservative/conserved lncRNA-sORF encoding a small peptide.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state psep3 knockout decreased growth rate and altered filament branching, and PSEP3 overexpression decreased growth and caused browning under some conditions.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: MS translation evidence, overexpression lines, CRISPR/Cas9 knockouts, and morphology/growth assays are all described.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The summary accurately reflects Results: 57-aa conserved lncRNA-encoded peptide; knockout reduced growth/altered branching; overexpression reduced growth and caused browning on medium without ammonium tartrate.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results mention BCD medium, medium without glucose and ammonium tartrate, and medium without ammonium tartrate for PSEP3 phenotypes.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper says translation of these selected SEPs was confirmed by several unique peptides, but does not clearly specify a full three-tissue pattern for PSEP3; this wording is appropriately cautious.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Same as above: translation was confirmed by several unique peptides, while a specific all-tissue pattern is not claimed.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods describe overexpression in pPLV27/pPLV-Hpa-3FR and do not explicitly name the promoter; the field reflects that.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Overexpression caused decreased growth and much protonemal tissue turned brown.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The notation psep3 for knockout and PSEP3 for overexpression is consistent with the paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly identifies PSEP25 as Pp3c25_sORF1000.

Source_Species.reason: The peptide was characterized in moss.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests were done in moss.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experiments were performed in Physcomitrella patens.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The same 'Gransden 2004' material is the source.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper describes Pp3c25_sORF1000 as a conserved lncRNA-sORF encoding a small peptide.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state psep25 knockout decreased growth rate, altered protonemal architecture, and increased leafy shoots under some conditions; overexpression slightly decreased growth and increased leafy shoots on medium without ammonium tartrate.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: MS evidence, overexpression, CRISPR/Cas9 knockout, and growth/morphology phenotyping are all directly described.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points match the Results, including 61-aa length, all-three-cell-type detection, and condition-dependent effects of knockout/overexpression.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results mention BCDAT medium, medium without glucose but with ammonium tartrate, and BCD medium without ammonium tartrate.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Pp3c25_sORF1000 is explicitly listed among three SEPs common to gametophores, protonemata, and protoplasts.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods report overexpression construct in pPLV27/pPLV-Hpa-25FR and do not explicitly state promoter identity.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results explicitly say PSEP25-overexpressing lines displayed a slight decrease in growth rate.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly says Pp3c25_sORF1000 was one of three lncRNA-sORF peptides common to all three cell types and confirmed by several unique endogenous peptides.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly identifies PSEP18 as Pp3c18_sORF57.

Source_Species.reason: This peptide was studied in moss.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses were done in moss.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experiments were performed in Physcomitrella patens.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify Physcomitrella patens subsp. patens 'Gransden 2004'.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The source line is the same 'Gransden 2004'.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper describes PSEP18 as a peptide encoded by an lncRNA-sORF.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state psep18 knockout caused a slight decrease in plant diameter, with only one KO line showing significant growth reduction, and overexpression significantly decreased growth rate.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The study used MS confirmation, overexpression, CRISPR/Cas9 knockout, and morphology/growth phenotyping.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The summary matches Results: 40-aa peptide, detected in all three cell types, overexpression reduced growth, knockout effects were mild except one start-codon deletion line.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results describe phenotypes on BCD medium with 0.5% glucose and on medium with glucose and without ammonium tartrate.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Pp3c18_sORF57 is explicitly reported as common to all three cell types.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods describe overexpression construct in pPLV27/pPLV-Hpa-18FR and do not explicitly state promoter identity.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Overexpression significantly decreased growth rate, which is a clear negative tradeoff.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results note that one KO-1 line had deletion of a start codon and that possible NMD relevance requires more detailed investigation.
